diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Light-headedness with hyperventilation fits an anxiety picture.
Acute dizziness in an older adult keeps stroke in play.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unilateral deficit / facial droop / dysarthria / LOC

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of classic anterior-circulation signs lowers, but does not exclude, stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetric perioral/finger paraesthesiae with hyperventilation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric perioral/peripheral paraesthesiae from respiratory alkalosis strongly support hyperventilation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dizziness settles at rest; no persistent vertigo, headache, diplopia or dysphagia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of posterior-circulation features lowers stroke probability but does not exclude it in an older adult.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day in-person assessment with full neuro exam and observations
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Given age and acute dizziness, in-person examination is the key step telehealth cannot provide.
Arrange scheduled B12 injections and monitoring electively.
